member.office_address: 207 Cannon House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0304

form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
To assist Congressman Stanton in advocating for the federal priorities and programs that most benefit Arizona, we request that you complete this form for Fiscal Year 2027 programmatic requests only.

Please submit one form per request. Enter "N/A" for any required response that is not applicable. Note that your request may be shared with relevant individuals and organizations during the appropriations process.

Please do not use this form for Community Project Funding (CPF) requests. If you would like to submit a CPF request, please contact Jennifer Arnold (jennifer.arnold@mail.house.gov). 

Deadline for Submission - March 6 at 9pm AZ time. We will update this form when guidance has been released from the Appropriations Committee. Please direct any questions to: Appropriations.AZ04@mail.house.gov

sections.description: Basic information about the organization and individual submitting the request.

fields.label: Point of contact (if different from above name)
Point of contact email (if different from above email)
Which Appropriations Subcommittee does this request fall under?
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs and Related Agencies
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs (previously SFOPS)
Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA's budget is determined by the Commerce, Justice, Science (CJS) subcommittee.

fields.label: Is this same request being made to other Representatives or Senators? If so, who?
fields.draft_value: Yes, this request is being made to other members of Congress, including Representatives Don Bacon and Judy Chu.
fields.rationale: The reference values indicate an existing house letter led by Reps. Bacon and Chu.

fields.label: If you are submitting more than one request to our office, please rank the priority of this request below:
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is specific to the individual submitter's internal strategy and other potential requests to this office.

fields.label: Request Type
fields.rationale: The reference context identifies this as a programmatic funding request for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Department and/or Agency (ex: U.S. Department of Agriculture)
fields.rationale: The pre-computed reference values identify the agency as NASA.

fields.label: Division/Bureau/Office (ex: Food and Nutrition Service)
fields.rationale: The reference values list the Science Mission Directorate as the specific office within NASA.

fields.label: Account (ex: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
fields.rationale: The reference values explicitly identify the account as 'Science'.

fields.label: Subaccount/Activity (if applicable)
fields.rationale: This is a topline programmatic request for the entire Science Mission Directorate account; specific subaccounts are not specified in the ask context.

fields.label: Short Description of Program (1-2 sentences)
fields.draft_value:
* NASA's Science Mission Directorate (SMD) conducts robotic exploration across the solar system, studies the Sun and Earth, and maps the deep universe.
* It manages a portfolio of over 100 missions providing critical data for planetary defense, weather forecasting, and national scientific competitiveness.
fields.rationale: Based on standard NASA SMD mission scope and advocacy talking points regarding scientific exploration and national leadership.

fields.label: Funding Level
options: Support total funding level of: $ (indicated in next question)
Support the FY27 President's Budget Request
Funding should be increased (no specific amount)
fields.draft_value: Support total funding level of: $ (indicated in next question)
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific programmatic amount of $9,000,000,000, which exceeds both the FY26 enacted level and the FY25 president's budget request.

fields.label: FY27 Funding Level Requested
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding ask amount provided in the reference values.

fields.label: FY27 President's Budget Request (if applicable)
fields.rationale: The reference values state the FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released.

fields.label: FY26 Enacted Funding Level (if applicable)
fields.rationale: This value is provided in the reference values and requested for pre-fill.

fields.label: Does this programmatic request have a direct impact on Arizona?
fields.rationale: NASA Science has a significant impact on AZ-4, with over $103M in spending across recent years, including major contracts with Arizona State University and Kinetx Inc in Tempe.

fields.label: Short description of direct impact on Arizona (1-2 sentences)
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science invested over $103 million in AZ-04 institutions from FY2023-FY2025, supporting high-tech jobs and innovation at Arizona State University and Kinetx Inc.
* Local leaders spearhead the Psyche mission to a metal asteroid and provide essential flight navigation for the OSIRIS-REx and Lucy missions.
fields.rationale: Cites the $103.8M in recent district obligations and specific high-value projects like ASU's Psyche ($45M) and Kinetx's navigation work ($56M in contracts).

fields.label: If you would like to include additional information, please attached it here.
fields.rationale: File must be attached manually.

fields.label: Department and/or Agency (ex: U.S. Department of Agriculture)
fields.rationale: Duplicate field for agency; value remains NASA.

fields.label: Account (ex:  Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
fields.rationale: Duplicate field for account; value remains Science.

fields.label: Proposed Language (include the exact text)
fields.rationale: Standard programmatic funding request format using the specific $9 billion ask amount provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Purpose of the Proposed Language (What is the intended outcome of this request?)
fields.draft_value:
* Restores U.S. leadership in space science following FY2026 budget volatility and ensures workforce stability at local aerospace firms and universities.
* Enables NASA to execute peer-reviewed Decadal Survey priorities and remain competitive with China's rapidly expanding lunar and planetary programs.
* Supports a 3-to-1 economic return on investment by driving demand for cutting-edge R&D and high-skilled labor in Tempe and the broader Phoenix area.
fields.rationale: Integrates strategic advocacy points (leadership, China competition, economic ROI) with specific local workforce concerns.

fields.label: Is this issue mentioned in the President's FY27 budget?  If so, please indicate where/provide a brief description.
fields.draft_value: * The President's FY2027 Budget Request has not yet been released as of the time of this submission.
fields.rationale: The reference values explicitly state that the FY2027 President's Budget Request is currently unknown.

fields.label: Does this request have a direct impact on Arizona?
fields.rationale: NASA Science (SMD) has significant direct investment in AZ-04, including over $103 million in obligations between FY23-FY25. Top local recipients include Arizona State University (Psyche mission and STEM grants) and Kinetx Inc (OSIRIS-REx and Lucy mission support) in Tempe.

fields.label: Short description of direct impact on Arizona (1-2 sentences)
fields.draft_value:
* Sustained funding supports ASU's leadership of major missions and Kinetx Inc's critical flight dynamics services for asteroid sample returns.
* Funds local educational initiatives that expand NASA science access for Hispanic communities and build community-based STEM ecosystems in District 4.
fields.rationale: Highlights specific local grant work ($5.4M for Hispanic community access) and high-value contracts at ASU and Kinetx found in the spending context.

fields.label: If you would like to include additional information, please attach it here.
fields.rationale: File must be attached manually.

fields.label: Is this a DoD-related Congressionally Directed Medical Research Program (PRMRP) related request?
fields.rationale: This is a NASA civil space science request, not a Department of Defense medical research request.

fields.label: Defense Branch/Agency/Component (ex: Army - Military Personnel)
fields.rationale: This is a non-defense request falling under the CJS subcommittee.

fields.label: Please enter the budget line and title. (ex: Line 15 - Ground Technology)
fields.rationale: This field appears to be for defense-specific budget lines which do not apply to this NASA Science request.

fields.label: Please enter the Program Element (PE) number of the defense program for which you wish to increase funding. Every defense program has a PE number associated with it that is the seven- or eight-digit number followed by one or two letters, such as "PE 0601110D". Type a PE number below or "N/A" if not applicable
fields.rationale: The request is for a civilian NASA program, not a defense program with a PE number.

fields.label: Name of Program Request (ex: Materials and Manufacturing Research Technology)
fields.rationale: This is the standard program name for the requested funding as per reference values.

fields.label: Short Purpose/Description of Program (1-2 sentences)
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science uses space-based platforms to investigate fundamental questions about the universe while providing essential services like planetary defense and earth monitoring.
* The program serves as a primary driver of the U.S. aerospace workforce and innovation pipeline, supporting thousands of researchers and contractors nationwide.
fields.rationale: Summarizes the dual value of NASA Science as a scientific engine and an economic driver for the national aerospace workforce.

top_contracts.description: Igf::CL,ct::igf Psyche: journey to a metal world  this contract provides for work to be performed by the Principal Investigator in support of the phase a concept definition portion of the NASA discovery program 2014 Announcement of Opportunity (AO) (nnh14zda014o) solicitation dated november 5, 2014.  The mission Principal Investigator (PI), Dr. Linda elkins-tanton, of the school of Earth and space exploration at Arizona State University (ASU), is responsible for the success and scientific integrity of the mission.  As such, the PI shall lead the project, manage project resources, and direct all activities of the science team carried out in support of the mission.  Specifically, during phase a, the PI will lead the mission team in conducting the concept study report and associated site visit.  Should the mission be selected to continue into phase b, the PI will lead the team through the initiation of the phase b effort with a transition phase between notification of award and establishment of the phase b contract.   The PI is assisted by a deputy PI, Dr. James bell at Arizona State University.  Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) is the mission major partner for spacecraft development and mission implementation, and shall support the PI with a project team experienced in management, systems engineering, mission design, payload development, safety and mission assurance, navigation, and mission operations.  Specific objectives to address the phase a criteria and guidelines are:  - project management for the phase a contract - conduct and support of phase a project reviews - phase b/c/d/E spacecraft development and mission planning - phase b/c/d/E cost estimates - concept study report preparation - orals/site visit support by key personnel  this contract provides for work to be performed by the Principal Investigator in support of the phase a concept definition portion of the NASA discovery program 2014 Announcement of Opportunity (AO) (nnh14zda014o) solicitation dated november 5, 2014.  The mission Principal Investigator (PI), Dr. Linda elkins-tanton, of the school of Earth and space exploration at Arizona State University (ASU), is responsible for the success and scientific integrity of the mission.  As such, the PI shall lead the project, manage project resources, and direct all activities of the science team carried out in support of the mission.  Specifically, during phase a, the PI will lead the mission team in conducting the concept study report and associated site visit.  Should the mission be selected to continue into phase b, the PI will lead the team through the initiation of the phase b effort with a transition phase between notification of award and establishment of the phase b contract.   The PI is assisted by a deputy PI, Dr. James bell at Arizona State University.  Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) is the mission major partner for spacecraft development and mission implementation, and shall support the PI with a project team experienced in management, systems engineering, mission design, payload development, safety and mission assurance, navigation, and mission operations.  Specific objectives to address the phase a criteria and guidelines are:  - project management for the phase a contract - conduct and support of phase a project reviews - phase b/c/d/E spacecraft development and mission planning - phase b/c/d/E cost estimates - concept study report preparation - orals/site visit support by key personnel
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Developing the Psyche mission concept to explore a metal-rich asteroid.

top_contracts.description: The contractor shall provide the necessary personnel, facilities, services, and materials to design, code, integrate and test the OSIRIS-REx flight dynamics system to support the OSIRIS-REx launch and flight operations to retrieve a sample of the neo and return the sample to Earth. After launch, kinetx shall provide operations support for 30 days. The scope of this SOW covers phase c/d of the OSIRIS-REx life cycle. This work shall be performed in accordance with the requirements of this document and all attachments to the contract.
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Supporting OSIRIS-REx flight dynamics for asteroid sample return.

top_contracts.description: Lucy flight dynamics systems navigational phase b/c/d/E
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Guiding precise navigation systems for the Lucy asteroid mission.

top_grants.description: Engaging hispanic communities in authentic NASA science: broadening participation in science activation through local partnerships and national networks (engaging hispanic communities)
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Expanding authentic NASA science access for Hispanic communities.

top_grants.description: Sciact STEM ecosystems to broaden participation in authentic STEM learning: connecting subject matter experts  communities  and learners of all ages ("sciact STEM ecosystems")
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing community-based STEM ecosystems for authentic learning experiences.

top_grants.description: Our proposed cubesat observatory  the star-planet activity research cubesat (SPARCS)  will be the first mission to provide the timedependent spectral slope
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Building the SPARCS CubeSat to track time-varying star-planet activity.
Analyzing the makeup of distant, rocky worlds.

top_grants.description: In 2002  we were granted 110 hours gto time for a JWST medium-deep survey. With hst and Spitzer experience 14 years later  we redefine this survey to image~18 nircam fields in 7 filters to ab<~ 29 29.3 mag  yielding 140 000 galaxies at 0<~ z<~ 12 15 in 0.05 deg2 (>~ 30 hudfs!). We will study galaxy assembly + super-massive black-hole growth at z 0 7  including object variability on months years timescales. We study the reionizing population at z 2 8  and use the best lensing targets to optimize our study of first light objects at z 10 15. Our science goals are: 1. Galaxy assembly; 2. Super-massive black-hole growth; 3. Reionization; 4. First light; 5. Study the best lensing clusters to find JWST objects.     Map&remove straylight gradients  and quantify sample completeness in random field&cluster images: we will: a) use hudf and hff data to simulate  map and remove a range of straylight amplitudes and gradients that could affect random deep fields (DFS) and cluster fields with JWST; b) determine accurate completeness corrections vs. Galaxy flux  size  wavelength  and location in JWST dfs of various depth  and in cluster fields with their known galaxy cluster+icl foreground; c) determine the cluster selection functions by folding lensing maps with simulated background images  yielding simulated lensed images and their completeness corrections for lensed sources  which we show can be factors 2 3; and d) model cosmic variance in hierarchical models at z 6 15. We will correct the lfs for these completeness functions. We will then use a bayesian code to determine the optimal set of lf evolution parameters from the available JWST data to quantify how galaxies assembled since z<~ 15.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Quantifying early galaxy assembly using JWST deep field data.

top_grants.description: Seventy-five billion m dwarfs in our galaxy host at least one small planet in the Habitable Zone (HZ). The stellar ultraviolet (UV) radiation from m dwarfs is strong and highly variable  and impacts planetary atmospheric loss  composition and habitability. These effects are amplified by the extreme proximity of their hzs (0.1 0.4 au). JWST will characterize hz m dwarf planets and attempt the first spectroscopic search for life beyond the solar system. Knowing the uv environments of m dwarf planets will be crucial to understanding their atmospheric composition and a key parameter in discriminating between biological and abiotic sources for observed biosignatures. The uv flux emitted during the super-luminous premain sequence phase of m stars drives water loss and photochemical o2 buildup for terrestrial planets within the hz. This phase can persist for up to a billion years for the lowest mass m stars. Afterwards  uv-driven photochemistry during the main sequence phase strongly affects a planet S atmosphere  could limit the planet S potential for habitability  and may confuse studies of habitability by creating false chemical biosignatures. Our proposed cubesat observatory will be the first mission to provide the time-dependent spectral slope  intensity and evolution of m dwarf stellar uv radiation. These measurements are crucial to interpreting observations of planetary atmospheres around low-mass stars. Mission: the star-planet activity research cubesat (SPARCS) will be a 6u cubesat devoted to monitoring 25 m stars in two uv bands: sparcs far-uv (S- fuv: 153 171 nm) and sparcs near-uv (s-nuv: 260  300 nm). For each target  sparcs will observe continuously between one and three complete stellar rotations (4 45 DAYS) over a mission lifetime of 2 years. A uv characterization survey of m dwarfs  the most common of planet hosts  is a perfect experiment for a cubesat: -- uv astronomy cannot be done from the ground because of Earth S atmospheric absorption. -- photometry of nearby sources is an efficient use of a small aperture. -- unlike the hst  whose time is shared among many instruments and programs  a cubesat can provide dedicated space-based long-term monitoring in the uv. Technology: sparcs will advance uv detector technology by flying high Quantum Efficiency (QE)  uv-optimized detectors developed at JPL. These  delta-doped  detectors have a long history of deployment demonstrating greater than 5x the sensitivity of the detectors used by galex. Sparcs will pave the way for their application in missions like luvoir or habex. Education: the sparcs research program will train future scientists and mission leaders by mentoring five undergraduate students  three graduate students  and two post-doctoral scholars throughout all aspects of the mission  including engineering  science  data management and outreach. Relevance to NASA: the sparcs mission will address NASA S goals of identifying the characteristics and distribution of potentially Habitable environments  including hz planet hosts like proxima and trappist-1. Sparcs will also be capable of  targetof- opportunity  uv observations of NASA S TESS yield of rocky planets in m dwarf hzs  some of the first hz planets to be spectroscopically characterized by JWST. Sparcs can provide the needed uv context for the interpretation of transmission and emission spectra of these potentially Habitable planets. Further into the future  sparcs results will inform the target strategy for the enormous telescopic investments in exoplanet science of luvoir or habex. Sparcs  technology will fill a gap in NASA S capabilities to observe low-mass stellar/planetary systems in the fuv and nuv. Hst S uv capabilities will not last much later than 2019  with future opportunities (E.g.  Luvoir) not arriving until sometime after 2035. The detector technology of this cubesat will play a crucial role in these and interim uv-capable missions.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Providing crucial UV context for interpreting JWST exoplanet atmospheric data.
